behavior_summary: When the player 'looks at' or examines the POLE, the script displays a single short description line ('pole ') as a bark and then suspends the process.
There are no conditionals, menus, flag reads/writes, or external calls; the behavior is a single-step presentation of text followed by suspend.
The routine is implemented as a process-style handler (U8P_POLE::look/examine handler) and appears intended only to provide a short in-world label/description for the POLE object.
notable_control_flow: U8P_POLE::look: immediate linear flow — emit one Bark node with text 'pole ' then call suspend/terminate; no branching or loops.
open_questions: Whether U8P_POLE::look is ever invoked by entering a fast-area trigger (enterFastArea) for the object in addition to the explicit player 'U8P_POLE::look' action — inferred but not present in extracted nodes (confidence: low).
The trailing space in the bark text 'pole ' — intentional formatting vs. extraction artifact (confidence: low).

main_function.name: U8P_POLE::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short descriptive text shown when the player examines the POLE object and then suspend the process.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player uses the 'U8P_POLE::look' (examine) action on the POLE object.
Possibly invoked by an engine 'U8P_POLE::look' type process or fast-area entry for the object (inferred).

function_map.name: U8P_POLE::look
function_map.role: Examine/description handler that emits a single bark and suspends.
